ddls:
CREATE TABLE "components" (
  "component_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each electronic component */,
  "component_name" TEXT /* Name of the component */,
  "component_type" TEXT /* Type of the component (e.g., resistor, capacitor, IC) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the component */,
  "manufacturer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the component's manufacturer */,
  "date_added" TEXT /* The date when the component was added to the database */,
  "spec_sheet_link" TEXT /* A link to the component's specification sheet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parameters" (
  "param_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance parameter */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the component to which the parameter belongs */,
  "symbol" TEXT /* Symbol representing the parameter (e.g., TalL2adl) */,
  "characteristics" TEXT /* Description of the parameter characteristics */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum value of the parameter */,
  "typ_value" TEXT /* Typical value of the parameter */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum value of the parameter */,
  "units" TEXT /* Units of measurement for the parameter (e.g., ns) */,
  "tolerance" TEXT /* The tolerance range for the parameter */,
  "test_method" TEXT /* The method used to test the parameter */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* The date when the parameter was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("param_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parameters_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES components ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "units" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each unit */,
  "unit_name" TEXT /* Name of the unit (e.g., ns, volts) */,
  "unit_description" TEXT /* Description of the unit */,
  "conversion_factor" REAL /* A factor to convert the unit to a standard unit (e.g., seconds to nanoseconds) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "component_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each component type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the component type (e.g., IC, Passive Component) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the component type */,
  "standard" TEXT /* The industry standard or specification associated with the component type */,
  "average_lifespan" INTEGER /* The average lifespan of components of this type in years */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "historical_data" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical record */,
  "param_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the performance parameter */,
  "date_recorded" TEXT /* Date when the performance was recorded */,
  "value" REAL /* Recorded value of the parameter at that date */,
  "test_environment" TEXT /* The environment in which the parameter was tested (e.g., lab, production) */,
  "test_temperature" REAL /* The temperature at which the parameter was tested */,
  "test_pressure" REAL /* The pressure at which the parameter was tested */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_historical_data_param_id FOREIGN KEY ("param_id") REFERENCES parameters ("param_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, tester, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* The department the user belongs to */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* The user's phone number */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* The date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the parameter */,
  "param_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parameter being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the parameter was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* The IP address from which the access was made */,
  "device" TEXT /* The device used to access the system (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* The duration of the access session in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_param_id FOREIGN KEY ("param_id") REFERENCES parameters ("param_id")
)
